title: Canonical workflow
description: A canonical, machine-checkable route: the ordered capabilities that compose to satisfy one task_type, the verifier that gates the result, where the outcome is written, and how it terminates. Validated by scripts/check_architecture.py. Each step names a real skill and a capability that skill actually `provides` in skills/registry.yaml -- a workflow cannot reference a capability that does not exist.

task_type.description: The task class this route serves (e.g. 'scientific-hypothesis').

skill.description: Registry skill name providing this step.

capability.description: A token in that skill's capability.provides.

gates.description: Process/rule gates the route passes through (e.g. 'falsification-ladder(rule)', 'source_trace').

verifier.description: Skill name whose capability provides the falsification/verification verdict. Must be a step and must be in verification allow-list or provide a *.verdict capability.

requires.description: Outcome tokens that must exist for the route to be complete. Each must be produced by some step (capability.produces) or be the reserved token 'memory_destination'.

failure_sink.description: Where a rejected/falsified result is recorded (e.g. 'null_results').

memory_sink.description: Where a completed outcome is written (e.g. 'experiments/').

termination_condition.description: Explicit non-empty stop condition -- no route may run unbounded.

human_checkpoint.description: Required when risk_tier is Yellow/Red/Black: the human gate before the irreversible action.
